topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up, bright and insistent. It was from Mr. Harrison. My breath hitched, a sudden, sharp intake of air that felt too loud in the quiet library. I clicked it, expecting a syllabus update, but instead, I found himself on his personal feed. A picture of him, laughing, with sunlight catching the edge of his smile. My fingers froze over the screen. A warmth bloomed low in my stomach, spreading outwards, making my palms suddenly slick. He had such a relaxed aura online, a genuine ease that made my chest tighten. I stared, mesmerized by the casual way he interacted with others. Every caption felt like a secret invitation. I quickly minimized the window, my heart hammering a frantic rhythm against my ribs, a giddy, breathless feeling washing over me.

[story 2]

It happened during the late-night study session. I was scrolling aimlessly through my phone when I saw it—a photo of me, taken weeks ago during a group project, but somehow enhanced, framed perfectly against a backdrop that looked suspiciously like the faculty lounge. Mr. Davies’ handle was tagged. A strange, dizzying sensation took hold, making the fluorescent lights above my desk seem to hum with intensity. I couldn't look away. The way he framed the shot, the subtle angle of his head—it all felt charged. I reread the comments, trying to rationalize the sudden, overwhelming flutter in my chest. It wasn't just admiration; it was a deep, consuming pull. A slow, undeniable smile stretched across my face, even though I knew no one else was looking. I felt utterly captivated by this unexpected glimpse into his private world.

[story 3]

I was reviewing my notifications when I saw the tag. It was Mr. Evans. He had posted a short video—a clip of him tending his small garden—and I was in the comments section, a simple, admiring remark about the vibrancy of the greens. Moments later, a direct message appeared. It wasn't a formal reply; it was a simple, personalized acknowledgment. My hands trembled slightly as I read the words. The sheer unexpectedness of it sent a jolt straight through me. A flush crept up my neck, warm and undeniable. I felt a strange, heightened awareness of my own presence, as if I were suddenly standing under a spotlight. Every line of text seemed imbued with significance. I spent the next hour just staring at the screen, the quiet hum of the room fading into the background as my focus narrowed entirely onto the digital exchange.
